title: 🏆 Championship Run

How I combined Yahoo API automation,
     Monte Carlo simulation, and
     strategic tanking to win it all

title: 🏆 Championship Run
text:
Season Journey


  
    Weeks 1–5
    Injury Chaos Begins
    Top draft picks sidelined early. Thanks to the strong roster dominating FG%, REB, AST, and BLK categories, I reached rank 2 early.
  

  
    Weeks 6–18
    Multiple Injuries, Holding Position
    Roster never fully healthy for 18 straight weeks. Utilized strategic waiver wire moves on category-specific strengths of REB, AST, and low TOs to maintain standings at rank 9.
  

  
    Week 19 — Turning Point
    🔑 Player come back + KD + Raynaud Trade
    4 players come back from injury after All-star break, and acquired Kevin Durant and Maxime Raynaud at low cost thanks to season ending sale. Projection model flagged an immediate boost across 3PT, PTS, STL.
  

  
    Weeks 19–21
    Roster at Full Strength
    Jokić, Durant, and Paul George all healthy simultaneously for the first time. Weekly win probabilities climbed above 85%.
  

  
    Week 22 — The Tank
    🎲 Strategic Seed 8 Decision
    Identified that Jokić and Clingan had only 2 games in Week 23 due to NBA scheduling. Deliberately lost Week 21 to drop to Seed 8, avoiding a top-seed semi-final during the weakest week.
  

  
    Weeks 23–24
    🏆 Playoff Dominance
    Semi-finals won against a mid-seed opponent despite Jokić's limited games. Finals entered with full roster and 91.7% simulated win probability.

title: 🏆 Championship Run
text:
The Technical Stack

  
    🤖
    Yahoo API Automation
    Daily automated pulls of roster stats, injury flags, and matchup data
       via OAuth. Output pushed directly to Excel reports each morning.
  
  
    🎲
    Monte Carlo Simulation
    100,000 simulations per matchup using Poisson distributions per player
       per game. Generated category-level and overall win probabilities.
  
  
    📊
    Excel Matchup Board
    Auto-generated weekly Excel boards showing projected vs. actual stats,
       games remaining, injury status, and category breakdowns.
  
  
    🏥
    Injury Tracking
    ESPN injury feed integrated with roster data. Automated GTD/OUT flags
       with season-average fallbacks to keep projections realistic.
  
  
    ⚗️
    Last-30 Baseline Stats
    Rolling 30-day averages as projection baseline, with season-stat
       fallback for players returning from injury.
  
  
    🌲
    Games Remaining Model
    NBA schedule scraper estimated remaining games per team each week,
       driving volume-adjusted projections that raw averages miss.

title: 🏆 Championship Run
text:
The Strategic Tank
My two best players had a critical NBA scheduling problem in Week 23:

Nikola Jokić — elite across all 7 categories, but only 2 games in Week 23
Donovan Clingan — elite REB + BLK, but only 2 games in Week 23

This made Week 23 my weakest week of the playoffs regardless of seeding. The key question was: which opponent do I face in that weak week?


Code
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec

fig = plt.figure(figsize=(13, 6.5))
gs = gridspec.GridSpec(2, 1, hspace=0.7)

xs = [0.3, 4.2, 8.1]
week_labels = ['Week 22\n(Quarter-finals)', 'Week 23\n(Semi-finals)', 'Week 24\n(Finals)']
BOX_W, BOX_H = 3.5, 0.8

def draw_row(ax, title, title_color, boxes):
    ax.set_xlim(0, 12.5)
    ax.set_ylim(-0.05, 1.6)
    ax.axis('off')
    ax.text(0.2, 1.52, title, color=title_color,
            fontsize=10.5, fontweight='bold', va='top')

    for i, (label, sub, color) in enumerate(boxes):
        x = xs[i]
        rect = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch(
            (x, 0.45), BOX_W, BOX_H,
            boxstyle="round,pad=0.1",
            linewidth=1.3, edgecolor='#bbbbbb',
            facecolor=color
        )
        ax.add_patch(rect)
        ax.text(x + BOX_W/2,
                0.45 + BOX_H/2 + (0.1 if sub else 0),
                label, ha='center', va='center',
                color='white', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')
        if sub:
            ax.text(x + BOX_W/2,
                    0.45 + BOX_H/2 - 0.18,
                    sub, ha='center', va='center',
                    color='#eeeeee', fontsize=7.8)
        ax.text(x + BOX_W/2, 0.38, week_labels[i],
                ha='center', va='top',
                color='#666666', fontsize=7.5)
        if i < len(boxes) - 1:
            ax.annotate('',
                xy=(xs[i+1] - 0.05, 0.45 + BOX_H/2),
                xytext=(x + BOX_W + 0.05, 0.45 + BOX_H/2),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#888888', lw=1.5))

# PATH A: Seed 3-6
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0])
draw_row(ax1,
    title='PATH A — Seed 3 to 6  (rejected)',
    title_color='#c62828',
    boxes=[
        ('QF: vs Seed 7/8',    '✅ Jokić 4G — Win easily',       '#2e7d32'),
        ('SF: vs Seed 1 or 2', '❌ Jokić only 2G — Cannot win',  '#c62828'),
        ('Finals: if reached', '⚠️ Never gets here',             '#9e9e9e'),
    ]
)
ax1.text(12.3, 0.85, '~8%\nchamp\nprob',
         ha='right', va='center', fontsize=8.5,
         color='#c62828', fontweight='bold')

# PATH B: Seed 8
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1])
draw_row(ax2,
    title='PATH B — Seed 8  ✓ chosen',
    title_color='#2e7d32',
    boxes=[
        ('QF: vs Seed 1',      '⚠️ Jokić 4G — 50/50',           '#e65100'),
        ('SF: vs Seed 4 or 5', '✅ Jokić 2G — Still winnable',   '#1565c0'),
        ('Finals: Week 24',    '✅ Full roster — 91.7% win prob', '#1b5e20'),
    ]
)
ax2.text(12.3, 0.85, '~16%\nchamp\nprob',
         ha='right', va='center', fontsize=8.5,
         color='#2e7d32', fontweight='bold')

fig.suptitle(
    "The Tank Decision: Jokić's Week 23 Schedule Determined Everything",
    fontsize=11.5, fontweight='bold', y=1.01
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()










Round-by-round probability breakdown








Round
Seed 3–6 Path
Seed 8 Path (chosen)




Week 22 (QF)
✅ ~85% — vs easy Seed 7/8
⚠️ ~50% — vs Seed 1


Week 23 (SF)
❌ ~15% — Jokić 2G vs top seed
✅ ~65% — Jokić 2G vs mid seed


Week 24 (Finals)
⚠️ ~50% if reached
✅ ~92% — full roster


Championship probability
85% × 15% × 50% ≈ 6%
50% × 65% × 92% ≈ 30%




Tanking to Seed 8 roughly quintupled the championship probability.

title: 🏆 Championship Run
text:
Automation Pipeline


Core daily pipeline (run_manual.py)
def main():
    # 1. OAuth authentication with Yahoo API
    client = YahooClient(auth)

    # 2. Fetch current matchup context
    matchup = get_current_matchup_context(client, league_key, my_team_key)

    # 3. Pull roster data for both teams
    my_roster  = get_team_roster(client, matchup.my_team_key)
    opp_roster = get_team_roster(client, matchup.opponent_team_key)

    # 4. Last-30-day stats as baseline
    #    (falls back to season avg for returning injured players)
    baseline_stats = get_player_last_30d_stats(client, all_player_keys)

    # 5. Estimate remaining games per NBA team this week
    games_remaining = estimate_games_remaining_by_team(
        week_end=matchup.week_end,
        team_abbrs=all_team_abbrs
    )

    # 6. Build projections:
    #    actuals so far + remaining games × per-game avg
    my_proj  = build_player_projection_table(...)
    opp_proj = build_player_projection_table(...)

    # 7. Write Excel matchup board + injury tracking sheet
    write_excel_report(output_path, my_proj, opp_proj, ...)

title: 🏆 Championship Run
text:
Key Takeaways






TipWhat Made the Difference




Baseline quality — Last-30-day stats with injury-aware fallbacks gave realistic projections, not season averages skewed by early-season performance.
Games remaining model — Knowing a player has 4 games vs 2 games this week often matters more than per-game averages alone.
Simulation over gut feel — Monte Carlo revealed 3PM/TO were already lost before the week started, so focus shifted to winning the other 5 categories.
The tank was counter-intuitive — Conventional wisdom says chase the highest seed. The scheduling data showed the opposite was optimal.




Built with Python · Yahoo Fantasy API · Monte Carlo Simulation · Quarto

What I work on
I work at the intersection of data science and business decision-making. My focus areas include pricing analytics, demand forecasting, experimentation, customer segmentation, and text-based modeling. I care about building models that are interpretable, testable, and deployable under real-world constraints.

title: Codex NBA Fantasy — Agentic Matchup Analytics
text:
Why I built this
Weekly fantasy decisions — who to start, who to stream, who to drop — are usually made on vibes. This project replaces the vibes with a reproducible pipeline: pull the live league state from Yahoo, project the next ~7 days of head-to-head category math, and ship a single Excel workbook that tells me exactly where my matchup is fragile and which waiver pickups would actually move those categories.
It runs on demand or on a daily cron, and it’s been the backbone of my real fantasy season.

title: Codex NBA Fantasy — Agentic Matchup Analytics
text:
What it produces
Each run writes one Excel workbook with four sheets:
Matchup Board. Head-to-head category projections for me vs. my scheduled opponent across the nine standard Yahoo categories (FG%, FT%, 3PTM, PTS, REB, AST, ST, BLK, TO). Three time slices per category — Now (already locked in), Future (remaining games × projected per-game), and Total. Makes it explicit whether a lead is real or fragile.
Average Impact. Season-average impact per player, with a graceful fallback to prior-season averages when the current sample is too small (GP < 10). This avoids the classic “one good game makes a stretch waiver pickup look elite” trap.
Free Agents. Ranked waiver-wire candidates filtered by which categories I am actually losing in this matchup, scored by remaining games × per-game impact on those categories. Not generic rest-of-season rankings — pickups specifically tuned to flip the categories that matter this week.
Injury Tracking Lists. A persistent watchlist of injured / suspended / low-availability players, merged from Yahoo statuses, ESPN’s injury feed, and free-text player notes. Each row carries a return-rate score, expected return date, the keyword that triggered the pickup (“questionable”, “doubtful”, “G-League”, “minutes restriction”…), a recency bucket, and per-game category impact. The sheet is reloaded from the previous run on every refresh, so dropoffs and upgrades day-over-day are visible.

Module map:







File
Responsibility




src/config.py
Loads and validates .env settings


src/yahoo_auth.py
OAuth 2.0 flow, token persistence, automatic refresh


src/yahoo_client.py
Thin Yahoo Fantasy API wrapper with retry + tree-walking helpers


src/data_fetch.py
League / team / roster / stat fetchers and the schedule heuristic


src/projections.py
Per-player projections, matchup category math, free-agent scoring


src/external_injuries.py
ESPN injury feed + normalized name matching


src/output_writer.py
openpyxl Excel writer with conditional formatting


run_manual.py
Main entrypoint — full report


run_matchup_board.py
Matchup-board-only entrypoint (custom week / opponent)


run_week22_playoff_boards.py
Playoff-week variant for two arbitrary teams

title: Codex NBA Fantasy — Agentic Matchup Analytics
text:
Design notes
Stats are computed, not scraped. All projections come from the Yahoo Fantasy API. ESPN is used only for injury context.
Time-aware projections. Splitting each category into Now / Future / Total was the most useful UX choice in the whole project. A 12–11 lead in 3PTM looks different when Now is 10–4 vs. when Now is 2–9 — and the board makes that obvious without me having to mentally subtract.
Sample-size guardrails. Per-game rates fall back to season averages or prior-season averages when fewer than two games exist in the rolling window. Prevents waiver candidates from looking artificially elite off a single hot night.
Injury intelligence as a first-class feature. The Injury Tracking sheet isn’t just an INJ tag — it scores each watched player on (1) keyword urgency parsed from Yahoo player notes, (2) ESPN’s published expected return date, (3) recency of the news, and (4) per-category fantasy impact. Names listed in MANUAL_TRACK_PLAYERS are pinned regardless of status.
Name normalization. Yahoo, ESPN, and the NBA schedule feed disagree on accents, suffixes, and punctuation. external_injuries.normalize_name makes the joins reliable.
Token hygiene. Secrets in .env, OAuth tokens in data/yahoo_tokens.json, both gitignored.

title: Codex NBA Fantasy — Agentic Matchup Analytics
text:
Quickstart
git clone <repo-url>
cd codex-nba-fantasy-analytics
pip install -r requirements.txt
cp .env.example .env       # then fill in Yahoo client id/secret + league + team
python run_manual.py       # one-time OAuth on first run
Custom-week scouting:
python run_matchup_board.py --week 23                                    # auto-detect opponent
python run_matchup_board.py --week 24 --my-team-id 1 --opponent-team-id 12
python run_matchup_board.py --week 25 --team-a-id 3 --team-b-id 8        # any two teams
Optional daily cron:
./install_daily_cron.sh         # default 08:00
./install_daily_cron.sh 7 30    # custom 07:30
./remove_daily_cron.sh
